frame_analyses.description: Black screen indicating start of video
Laparoscopic instruments positioned over FLS trainer with Penrose drain visible on pink gauze
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Setup appears proper with drain positioned on marked gauze

frame_analyses.description: Needle holder grasping suture, beginning placement through first mark on drain
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good needle control, suture appears to be 2-0 silk

frame_analyses.description: Suture being passed through drain at first marking location
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Placement appears accurate to the marked location

frame_analyses.description: Working on second suture placement through drain
Beginning knot tying sequence, creating first throw
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Appears to be creating surgeon's knot (double throw) as required
Double throw technique being executed properly

frame_analyses.description: Working on second throw with hand switching
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Hand switching appears to be occurring as required
Both suture ends being trimmed appropriately

frame_analyses.description: Final view of completed knot on drain
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Knot appears secure and properly positioned

knot_assessments.notes: Single throw with proper hand switching
Final single throw completing the sequence

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Drain slit appears properly closed with no visible gaps

technique_summary: Excellent execution of FLS Task 5 with proper surgeon's knot formation, appropriate hand switching between throws, and accurate suture placement. The knot appears secure and the drain is properly closed.
improvement_suggestions: Continue practicing to reduce overall completion time
Work on minimizing any small deviations in suture placement
